Empires & Puzzles Buster Review: The 3-Star Titan Slayer

Don't let the size fool you. This grumpy, tattooed garden gnome packs a punch that rivals the biggest heroes in the game. Buster isn't just a 3-star hero; he's a 'Mini-Franz' that can revolutionize your Titan scores and Challenge Event rankings. We break down why this Slow mana brawler deserves a spot on your roster, even if his stats are starting to show their age.
Buster: The Grumpy Garden Gnome with a Heavy Hand

Buster entered the arena in December 2022 as part of the Santa's Challenge (Winter) event. In a family dominated by jolly elves and festive reindeer, Buster stands out as the grumpy uncle who definitely spiked the eggnog. He is a
While "Slow" and "3-star" usually screams "feeder material" for advanced players, Buster breaks the mold. He is effectively a miniature version of the 4-star powerhouse

, bringing the coveted "Damage Increase" ailment to the 3-star rarity. This unique utility makes him relevant far beyond the typical lifespan of a Rare hero, earning him a spot on Titan teams fighting enemies ten times his size.

Let's be honest: Buster is hideous, and we love him for it. While the rest of the Winter family looks like they stepped out of a Hallmark movie, Buster looks like he stepped out of a bar fight in a garden center.
With a black eye, tattoos that look like they were done in a prison kitchen (is that an anchor?), and bandaged fists, he tells a story of a gnome who has
. He’s not here to wrap gifts; he’s here to wrap his knuckles around your face. It's a hilarious and gritty departure from the cutesy aesthetic of the Christmas event.
When we compare Buster to the
of 2025 and 2026, his age is showing.
Significantly lower than modern 3-star hitters like
(559). He doesn't hit particularly hard with tile damage.
This is his Achilles' heel. Modern 3-star heroes like
(524) have much higher bulk. Buster is paper-thin.
Average for his era, but dwarfed by the new standard where 3-stars push 900-1000+ HP (e.g.,
. He is fragile and relies entirely on his powerful Special Skill to justify his roster slot.
This skill is the reason you level Buster.
Decent for a 3-star, but not the main event.
He summons an Elf Minion for himself and nearby allies. This provides a small meat shield (essential for his survival) and a tiny bit of extra damage.
Note: In competitive events, minion animations can eat up precious seconds, which is a downside.
All damage the targets receive is increased. This is a separate multiplier from Defense Down (like Wilbur) and Elemental Defense Down (like Guardian Falcon). It stacks with both.
This is nearly identical to the mechanic that makes
a Titan god. While Franz covers all enemies and prevents status effects, Buster hits 3 (usually enough for a Titan) and boosts damage.
You need significant boosts to make him faster in normal play, but he is primarily used for Titans (Mana Potions) or Rush (set speed).
If you hit a target with Buster's ailment active, your subsequent tile damage and special skills do up to
If a tile usually hits a Titan for 1,000, with Defense Down (+50%) and Elemental Defense Down (+54%), it might hit for ~3,000. Add Buster's +40% multiplier on top of
final number, and you are looking at massive escalation.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Winter family bonus gives minions extra health and attack. Since Buster summons minions for himself and neighbors, this adds a bit of bulk. It's nice, but not game-changing.
Note: Some newer Winter costumes introduced passives like "Frost on Minion Hit", but base Buster relies on the family bonus.
Useful for survival. Since Buster is incredibly fragile, a lucky dodge can be the difference between him keeping his debuff active or getting one-shot by a slash attack.

Upon release, Buster was immediately identified as a "Must Have" for players who didn't own Franz, or for hitting Green Titans specifically. He filled a gap in the 3-star rarity that didn't exist before (Damage Amplification).
While his stats have fallen behind, his
has not. The specific mechanic of "Increase Damage Taken" is rare. Even in 2026, there are very few heroes who do this, and almost none at the 3-star level.
He is still a staple for Green Titans (up to 14*) and Mythic Titans, provided you can keep him alive with items (Time Stops/Potions).
Essential for high scores in the Rare tier of Challenge Events to maximize damage on bosses.
Outside of 3-Star Rush Tournaments, he is too slow and too squishy for modern PvP.
This is a point of contention in the community. Buster is a utility hero who needs to survive, but his class is
Buster's base defense (451) is abysmal. A 14* Titan can look at him funny and he will die. This path, combined with Limit Breaking, gives him the best chance to survive a slash attack.
Maximizes tile damage for Challenge Events where you replay stages until you get a board where enemies never hit you anyway.
He becomes made of glass. A single sneeze from a Titan will kill him.
path is recommended. You bring Buster for the
, not his personal damage. If he dies before he casts, he is useless. Prioritize keeping him on his feet.
The +40% damage taken debuff is one of the strongest offensive tools in the game.
Vital component of high-scoring Green Titan teams.
Dominates damage potential in 3-star Challenge Events and Alliance Quests.
Effect stacks with standard Def Down (Wilbur/Gormek) and Elemental Def Down (Guardian Falcon/C.Marjana).
Extremely low base defense makes him hard to keep alive against high-level content.
Without Rush rules or Mana Potions, he is too slow for most raids.
Summons minions which eat up time during timed events (though they help survival).
Unlike Franz, he doesn't hit all enemies, so you must aim carefully on wave stages or 5-enemy raids.
Specifically Green Titans and Mythic Titans. He replaces standard attackers to boost the team's total output.
Essential for pushing for top 100/1000 scores.
Specifically 3-Star Rush. His slow speed becomes Very Fast, and his impact is devastating.
Use Mana Potions to fire everyone. Buster's ailment multiplies the damage created by the other defense downs. The tile damage becomes astronomical.
While mixing colors (Nordri is Blue) is usually bad, in Rare events, stacking the three types of Defense Down (General + Elemental + Buster's Dmg Inc) leads to one-shotting bosses.
Any enemy that cleanses ailments (e.g., Faiez) wipes Buster's value instantly.
Hitting a Green Elemental Reflect enemy (rare, but happens in some events) will kill him instantly due to high damage output.
. Do not feed him away. Even if you don't have the materials to level him yet, he is a unique tool that you will use for years on Titans. Focus on keeping him alive with potions and defense emblems.
You likely already know his value, but consider
him solely for the stats. You don't need the Aether Power (Dodge) as much as you need the raw Defense/HP to let him survive a 14* Titan hit without needing a Time Stop every single turn. If you have
(Magic Tower) or other modern burners, Buster pairs beautifully to maximize burn ticks if the initial hit doesn't kill.
When Buster was released, the community immediately recognized his "Mini-Franz" status.
There was a significant split on emblem paths. As user
noted on the forums: "I went health over attack. I didn’t want him getting squished by the titan."
argued for full attack: "Buster can take one hit with a full sword path... One is enough, I wanna do max damage."

did the math on 14* Titans, revealing that with defense < 700, heroes usually die in one hit. Buster needs significant boosting (Limit Break + Defense Path + Legendary Troops) to survive consistently.

Buster is the definition of "punching above your weight class." He is an ugly, grumpy, slow 3-star hero who finds himself on teams alongside shiny 5-star legends because he brings something they don't: raw damage amplification.
He is not a hero you play for his own damage; he is a hero you play to make everyone else look like superheroes. If you care about Titans or Event Scores, Buster is not optional.
Keep him, emblem him for defense, and watch your Titan scores soar.
